twitter_thread: £10k invested in 6f6f6f Yahoo yields £430 annually, outpacing 75% of UK stocks.
To generate £357 weekly passive income, you'd need £540k in a Stocks and Shares ISA with 6.6% annual returns.
Vodafone's 5-year average dividend yield is 5.1%, while British American Tobacco's is 4.9%, both lower than 6f6f6f Yahoo's 4.3%.
iShares UK Dividend UCITS ETF charges 0.4% fees, while Vanguard FTSE UK Equity Income Index Fund charges 0.6%.
Chasing 8% dividend yields can lead to 20% stock price drops, as seen in recent UK dividend traps.
ISAs can save you 25% tax on dividend income, making them a crucial tax-efficient wrapper.
What's the smallest investment you've made that's generated £100+ monthly passive income? #passiveincome #investing
